talks.title: Why Your Brain Lies to You
talks.speaker: Dr. Sarah Okonkwo

questions.question: What is the name for the brain's tendency to seek patterns even in random data?
options: Apophenia
Pareidolia
Confabulation
Priming

questions.question: The 'change blindness' phenomenon shows that humans often fail to notice large changes in a scene when?
options: The scene is in low light
The change happens during a brief visual interruption
The observer is tired
The changed object is small

questions.question: What does the 'misinformation effect' in memory research demonstrate?
options: People remember traumatic events more accurately than mundane ones
Post-event information can alter a person's memory of the original event
Eyewitness testimony is reliable when given immediately after an event
False memories are only created under hypnosis

questions.question: Approximately how many bacterial cells live in one teaspoon of healthy soil?
options: A few hundred
About a million
Up to a billion
Around ten thousand

questions.question: What is mycorrhizae?
options: A soil-borne plant disease caused by bacteria
A symbiotic fungal network that connects plant roots and exchanges nutrients
The top layer of organic matter in a forest floor
A chemical process that converts nitrogen into plant-usable form

questions.question: What percentage of the world's food production depends on healthy topsoil?
options: Around 25%
About 50%
Roughly 70%
Over 95%

talks.title: Rockets Are Hard: A Love Letter to Failure
talks.speaker: Priya Nair

questions.question: What is the Tsiolkovsky rocket equation used to calculate?
options: The maximum altitude a rocket can reach
The relationship between a rocket's mass, exhaust velocity, and delta-v
The structural stress on a rocket during re-entry
The fuel combustion temperature needed for orbit

questions.question: Why is getting to orbit so much harder than simply reaching space (100km altitude)?
options: Orbital mechanics require far more fuel due to air resistance above 100km
You must reach orbital velocity (~7.8 km/s sideways), not just altitude
Space agencies add unnecessary safety margins to fuel calculations
The Van Allen belts require special shielding that adds mass

questions.question: What is 'specific impulse' and why do rocket engineers care about it?
options: The thrust-to-weight ratio of an engine at sea level
A measure of how efficiently a rocket engine uses propellant — higher is better
The maximum g-force a rocket structure can withstand during launch
The time delay between ignition and full thrust development

originStory.text:
Hi. I'm Justin Fritz — and I'm a Nerd Nite Boss.

That's a real title. The Nerd Nite organization gave it to me, and I take it very seriously.

Nerd Nite started in Boston in 2003. A guy named Matt Wasowski talked a bar into letting scientists give talks between band sets. It spread to over 100 cities worldwide.

It came to Fort Collins because of me and one other very important person: Senne Van Loon.

But first — trivia.

Senne and I built this thing from scratch.

Senne is the kind of person who hears "let's put on a nerdy event at a bar" and says yes immediately — full commitment, no hesitation. That energy is rare and it matters. You cannot build something like this alone.

As we grew, Jamie Fritz joined us. Jamie is the backbone — always behind the scenes, making everything work, never needing the spotlight. The kind of person every organization needs and rarely thanks enough.

Jamie has been onstage exactly once: to introduce Marybeth Arcodia. Which brings me to Marybeth.

Marybeth Arcodia was our host.

She was brilliant at it — warm, sharp, funny in exactly the right way for a room full of nerds on their third drink. She held the stage like she was born for it.

Marybeth eventually left Fort Collins for a faculty position in Miami. We were sad to lose her and enormously proud of her.

Hannah Bauer stepped up as host after Marybeth — and she's been incredible.

Here's the thing you need to understand about Nerd Nite: once you're a Boss, you're always a Boss. Marybeth is still a Boss. Distance doesn't change that.

You just played Nerdometer.

Every edition is built around a real Nerd Nite Fort Collins event — the actual talks, the actual speakers, the real nerdy obsessions people bring to a bar and share with strangers.

Senne. Jamie. Marybeth. Hannah. Every speaker who ever trusted a room full of people with the thing they love most.

All Bosses. Always.

Come be part of it.

— Justin Fritz, Nerd Nite Fort Collins Boss
